title: Magnet assembly
abstract: There is provided a method of manufacturing a magnet assembly for a transducer. The method comprises positioning the magnet assembly at least partially between a first magnetization coil and a second magnetization coil and magnetizing the magnet assembly by: (i) passing a first current through the first magnetization coil to generate a first magnetic field to magnetize a first magnetizable portion of the magnet assembly, the first magnetic field extending in a first direction, and (ii) passing a second current through the second magnetization coil to generate a second magnetic field to magnetize a second magnetizable portion of the magnet assembly, the second magnetic field extending in a second direction opposite to the first direction.
claims:
Claims 1. A method of manufacturing a magnet assembly for a transducer, the method comprising: positioning the magnet assembly at least partially between a first magnetization coil and a second magnetization coil, wherein the magnet assembly defines a longitudinal axis, and the longitudinal axis is arranged parallel to a winding axis of the first magnetization coil and a winding axis of the second magnetization coil; and magnetizing the magnet assembly by: passing a first current through the first magnetization coil to generate a first magnetic field to magnetize a first magnetizable portion of the magnet assembly, the first magnetic field extending in a first direction; and passing a second current through the second magnetization coil to generate a second magnetic field to magnetize a second magnetizable portion of the magnet assembly, the second magnetic field extending in a second direction opposite to the first direction. 2. The method of claim 1. wherein the first and second currents are passed through the first and second magnetization coils, respectively, at substantially the same time. 3. The method of claim 1 or 2, wherein the first and second currents are substantially the same. 4. The method of any preceding claim, further comprising: arranging the first magnetization coil relative to the second a magnetization coil, such that one of: (i) at the first magnetization coil, the first direction points towards the second magnetization coil, and at the second magnetization coil the second direction points towards the first magnetization coil; or (ii) at the first magnetization coil, the first direction points away from the second magnetization coil, and at the second magnetization coil the second direction points away from the first magnetization coil. 5. The method of any preceding claim, wherein one of: the first magnetizable portion and the second magnetizable portion form a monolithic magnetizable element; or the magnet assembly comprises a first magnetizable element and a second magnetizable element affixed to the first magnetizable element, the first magnetizable element comprising the first magnetizable portion and the second magnetizable element comprising the second magnetizable portion, and wherein the method further comprises: providing the first magnetizable element, providing the second magnetizable element and affixing the first magnetizable element to the second magnetizable element. 6. The method of claim 5, wherein: magnetizing the first magnetizable portion forms a first magnetized portion of the magnet assembly; magnetizing the second magnetizable portion forms a second magnetized portion of the magnet assembly; and the magnet assembly, after being magnetized by the first and second magnetization coils, comprises: a first end that has a same polarity as a second end of the magnet assembly, the first and second ends being spaced apart along the longitudinal axis; and a region between the first and second ends; and and the method comprises during the magnetizing, forming the region with a particular length, the length being measured in a direction along the longitudinal axis, by controlling at least one of: a magnetic reluctance external to the magnet assembly adjacent the region; and a magnetic flux through the region. 7. The method of claim 6, wherein controlling the magnetic flux through the region comprises at least one of: controlling a distance between the first and second magnetization coils along the longitudinal axis, wherein the length of the region is based on the distance; controlling magnitudes of the first and second currents, wherein the length of the region is based on the magnitudes; 

 arranging at least a third magnetization coil between the first and second magnetization coils, the third magnetization coil having a winding axis that is substantially perpendicular to the winding axes of the first and second magnetization coils, wherein the length of the region is based on a cross-sectional width of the third magnetization coil, the cross-sectional width measured parallel to the longitudinal axis; and arranging a permanent magnet between the first and second magnetization coils, the permanent magnet having a magnetic axis that is substantially perpendicular to the winding axes of the first and second magnetization coils, wherein the length of the region is based on a cross-sectional width of the magnet, the cross- sectional width measured parallel to the longitudinal axis. 8. The method of claim 6 or 7, wherein controlling the magnetic reluctance external to the magnet assembly adjacent the region comprises: arranging magnetizable material between the first and second magnetization coils and at least partially around the magnet assembly, such that the first magnetic field flows through the first magnetizable portion and through the magnetizable material and the second magnetic field flows through the second magnetizable portion and through the magnetizable material, wherein the length of the region is based on a cross-sectional width of the magnetizable material, the cross- sectional width measured parallel to the longitudinal axis. 9. The method of any of claims 5 to 8, wherein: the magnet assembly, after being magnetized by the first and second magnetization coils, comprises: a first end that has a same polarity 7 as a second end of the magnet assembly, the first and second ends being spaced apart along the longitudinal axis; and a region between the first and second ends; and the method further comprises forming the magnet assembly such that at least one of: a through hole extends through the region; and the region includes a void. 10. The method of any of claims 1 to 4, wherein: 

 the magnet assembly comprises a first magnetizable element, a second magnetizable element and a spacer element affixed to and arranged between the first and second magnetizable elements; and the first magnetizable element comprises the first magnetizable portion and the second magnetizable element comprises the second magnetizable portion; and the method further comprises: providing the first magnetizable element, the second magnetizable element and the spacer element; affixing the first magnetizable element to a first side of the spacer element; and affixing the second magnetizable element to a second side of the spacer element. 11. The method of claim 10, wherein providing the first and second magnetizable elements comprises providing first and second magnetizable elements having substantially the same magnetic coercivityâ. 12. The method of any preceding claim, further comprising: positioning a second magnet assembly at least partially between the first magnetization coil and the second magnetization coil, and simultaneously magnetizing the magnet assembly and the second magnet assembly. 13. The method of claim 12, wherein: positioning the magnet assembly at least partially between the first magnetization coil and the second magnetization coil comprises holding the magnet assembly in a first holder of a fixture, the fixture arranged at least partially between the first magnetization coil and the second magnetization coil; and positioning the second magnet assembly at least partially between the first magnetization coil and the second magnetization coil comprises holding the second magnet assembly in a second holder of the fixture. 14. The method of any preceding claim, further comprising: after positioning the magnet assembly at least partially between the first magnetization coil and the second magnetization coil and prior to magnetizing the magnet assembly, moving at least one of: 

 the first magnetization coil towards the second magnetization coil; or the second magnetization coil towards the first magnetization coil. 15. A magnetization device for magnetizing a magnet assembly having a longitudinal axis, the magnetization device comprising: a first magnetization coil having a winding axis configured to be arranged parallel to the longitudinal axis and configured to generate a first magnetic field for magnetizing a first magnetizable portion of the magnet assembly, the first magnetic field extending in a first direction; a second magnetization coil having a winding axis configured to be arranged parallel to the longitudinal axis, the second magnetization coil being spaced apart from the first magnetization coil and configured to generate a second magnetic field for magnetizing a second magnetizable portion of the magnet assembly, the second magnetic field extending in a second direction opposite to the first direction; and one or more current sources configured to apply: a first current to the first magnetization coil to generate the first magnetic field; and a second current to the second magnetization coil to generate the second magnetic field. 16. The magnetization device of claim 15, wherein the one or more current sources are configured to apply the first and second currents to the first and second magnetization coils, respectively, at substantially the same time. 17. The magnetization device of claim 15 or 16, wherein the one or more current sources are configured to apply the first and second currents to the first and second magnetization coils, respectively, such that they have substantially the same current. 18. The magnetization device of any of claims 15 to 17, wherein the first magnetization coil is configured to generate the first magnetic field having a first field strength at the first magnetizable portion and the second magnetization coil is configured to generate 

the second magnetic field having a second field strength at the second magnetizable portion, the first and second field strengths being substantially equal. 19. The magnetization device of any of claims 15 to 18, wherein the device is configured to receive the magnet assembly at least partially between the first and second magnetization coils, and wherein, in use, one of: (i) at the first magnetization coil, the first direction points towards the second magnetization coil, and at the second magnetization coil the second direction points towards the first magnetization coil; or (ii) at the first magnetization coil, the first direction points away from the second magnetization coil, and at the second magnetization coil the second direction points away from the first magnetization coil. 20. The magnetization device of any of claims 15 to 19, wherein the device further comprises at least one of: at least a third magnetization coil arranged between the first and second magnetization coils, the third magnetization coil having a winding axis that is substantially perpendicular to the winding axes of the first and second magnetization coils; and magnetizable material arranged between the first and second magnetization coils, the magnetizable material being configured to extend at least partially around the magnet assembly, such that in use, the first magnetic field flows through the first magnetizable portion and through the magnetizable material and the second magnetic field flows through the second magnetizable portion and through the magnetizable material. 21. The magnetization device of any of claims 15 to 20, wherein the device further comprises a fixture arranged at least partially between the first and second magnetization coils, the fixture comprising a first holder and a second holder, the first holder being configured to hold the magnet assembly, and the second holder being configured to hold a second magnet assembly for magnetization by the magnetization device. 22. The magnetization device of any of claims 15 to 21, wherein at least one of: 

 the first magnetization coil is moveable towards the second magnetization coil; or the second magnetization coil is moveable towards the first magnetization coil. 23. A system, comprising: the magnetization device of any of claims 15 to 22; and one or more workstations for assembling a magnet assembly. 24. A monolithic magnet having a longitudinal axis, the monolithic magnet comprising: a first magnetized portion having a first magnetic flux substantially orientated along the longitudinal axis; a second magnetized portion positioned along the longitudinal axis from the first magnetized portion and having a second magnetic flux substantially orientated along the longitudinal axis in the opposite direction to the first magnetic flux; and and a region positioned between the first magnetized portion and the second magnetized portion. 25. The monolithic magnet of claim 24, wherein the region includes a magnetic neutral zone. 26. The monolithic magnet of claim 24 or 25, wherein the region has a third magnetic flux substantially orientated perpendicular to the longitudinal axis. 27. The monolithic magnet of any of claims 24 to 26, wherein the region is located centrally between outer ends of the first and second magnetized portions. 28. The monolithic magnet of any of claims 24 to 27, further comprising: a third magnetized portion positioned along the longitudinal axis from the second portion and having a third magnetic flux substantially orientated along the longitudinal axis in the same direction to the first magnetic flux; and a second region positioned between the second magnetized portion and the third magnetized portion. 29. The monolithic magnet of any of claims 24 to 28, wherein at least one of: a through hole extends through at least part of a magnetic neutral zone of the region; and the region defines a void that includes at least part of the magnetic neutral zone. 30. The monolithic magnet of claim 29, wherein the through hole or void has a rotational symmetry order of at least 2 about the longitudinal axis. 31. The monolithic magnet of claim 29 or 30, wherein the through hole extends through the region in a direction perpendicular to the longitudinal axis and has a rotational symmetry order of 2. 32. A motor, comprising: the monolithic magnet of any of claims 24 to 31; and one or more voice coils. 33. A playback device, comprising the motor of claim 32. 34. A method of manufacturing a monolithic magnet, the method comprising: positioning a monolithic magnetizable element at least partially between a first magnetization coil and a second magnetization coil, wherein the monolithic magnetizable element defines a longitudinal axis, and the longitudinal axis is arranged parallel to a winding axis of the first magnetization coil and a winding axis of the second magnetization coil; magnetizing the monolithic magnetizable element to provide a monolithic magnet by: passing a first current through the first magnetization coil to generate a first magnetic field to magnetize a first magnetizable portion of monolithic magnetizable element; and passing a second current through the second magnetization coil to generate a second magnetic field to magnetize a second magnetizable portion of the monolithic magnetizable element; and 

 during the magnetizing, forming a region within the monolithic magnet having a particular length, the length being measured in a direction along the longitudinal axis, by controlling at least one of: a magnetic reluctance external to the monolithic magnet adjacent the region; and a magnetic flux through the region. 35. The method of claim 34, wherein controlling the magnetic flux through the region comprises controlling a distance between the first and second magnetization coils along the longitudinal axis, wherein the length of the region is based on the distance. 36. The method of claim 34 or 35, wherein controlling the magnetic flux through the region comprises controlling magnitudes of the first and second currents, wherein the length of the region is based on the magnitudes. 37. The method of any of claims 34 to 36, wherein controlling the magnetic flux through the region comprises arranging at least a third magnetization coil between the first and second magnetization coils, the third magnetization coil having a winding axis that is substantially perpendicular to the winding axes of the first and second magnetization coils, wherein the length of the region is based on a cross-sectional width of the third magnetization coil, the cross-sectional width measured parallel to the longitudinal axis. 38. The method of any of claims 34 to 37, wherein controlling the magnetic flux through the region comprises arranging a permanent magnet between the first and second magnetization coils, the permanent magnet having a magnetic axis that is substantially perpendicular to the winding axes of the first and second magnetization coils, wherein the length of the region is based on a cross-sectional width of the magnet, the cross-sectional width measured parallel to the longitudinal axis. 39. The method of any of claims 34 to 38, wherein controlling the magnetic reluctance external to the monolithic magnet adjacent the region comprises arranging magnetizable material between the first and second magnetization coils and at least partially around the monolithic magnetizable element, wherein the length of the region is based on a 

cross-sectional width of the magnetizable material, the cross-sectional width measured parallel to the longitudinal axis. 40. The method of any of claims 34 to 39, further comprising forming at least one of: a through hole through the region; or a void within the region. 41. The method of any of claims 34 to 40, further comprising: positioning the monolithic magnetizable element at least partially between the first magnetization coil and a third magnetization coil, the second magnetization coil being arranged between the first and third magnetization coils and extending around the monolithic magnetizable element, wherein a winding axis of the third magnetization coil is parallel to winding axes of the first and second magnetization coils; and passing a third current through the third magnetization coil to generate a third magnetic field to magnetize a third magnetizable portion of monolithic magnetizable element, the third magnetic field extending in the same direction as the first magnetic field.
